title: France Customs Regulations
description: What you can and cannot bring into France
content:
## Duty-Free Allowances
Travelers arriving from non-EU countries are entitled to the following duty-free allowances:

- Tobacco: 200 cigarettes OR 100 cigarillos OR 50 cigars OR 250g smoking tobacco
- Alcohol: 4L wine OR 16L beer OR 1L spirits over 22% volume OR 2L fortified/sparkling wine
- Perfume: 50g perfume and 250ml eau de toilette
- Other goods: Up to €430 value (air/sea travelers) or €300 value (other travelers)

## Restricted Items
- Meat and dairy products: Generally prohibited from non-EU countries
- Plants and plant products: Require phytosanitary certificate
- Endangered species products: Prohibited under CITES
- Counterfeit goods: Strictly prohibited
- Weapons: Require special authorization

## Prohibited Items
- Narcotics and illegal drugs
- Offensive weapons
- Counterfeit currency and securities
- pornographic materials involving minors
- Certain plants and plant products without certification

## Declaration Requirements
- Cash amounts over €10,000 must be declared
- High-value electronics, jewelry, and other valuables should be declared for insurance purposes
- Firearms and ammunition require prior authorization and declaration

## Agriculture Restrictions
- Fresh fruits and vegetables: Generally restricted from outside EU
- Seeds and bulbs: Require phytosanitary certificate
- Soil: Prohibited without special permit
- Live animals: Require health certificate and import permit

title: Japan Customs Regulations
description: What you can and cannot bring into Japan
content:
## Duty-Free Allowances
Travelers arriving in Japan are entitled to the following duty-free allowances:

- Tobacco: 200 cigarettes OR 50 cigars OR 250g tobacco
- Alcohol: 3 bottles (each approx 760ml) of alcoholic beverages
- Perfume: 2 ounces (approx 60ml) of perfume
- Other goods: Up to ¥200,000 value (approximately $1,500 USD)

## Restricted Items
- Firearms and ammunition: Strictly prohibited without prior authorization
- Sword blades: Require special permission for import
- Drugs: Certain medications require yakkan shoukou (import certificate)
- Plants and plant products: Require phytosanitary certificate
- Soil: Prohibited without special authorization
- Fresh fruit: Some varieties restricted due to pest concerns

## Prohibited Items
- Narcotics and stimulants (including certain prescription medications)
- Gunpowder and explosives
- Counterfeit goods
- Obscene materials
- Products infringing intellectual property rights
- Certain fresh fruits, vegetables, and meat products

## Declaration Requirements
- Cash, checks, and securities over ¥1,000,000 must be declared
- Separate declaration forms for restricted items like medications, plants, and animal products
- Unaccompanied baggage requires advance declaration

## Agriculture Restrictions
- Fresh fruits and vegetables: Many varieties prohibited or restricted
- Plants and seeds: Require phytosanitary certificate and may need post-entry quarantine
- Meat products: Generally prohibited from most countries
- Soil and organisms: Strictly controlled to prevent invasive species
- Live animals: Require health certificate, import permit, and may need quarantine
